title: Avoid use of system:masters group

input_row.title: Avoid use of system:masters group
input_row.description: The special group system:masters should not be used to grant permissions to any user or service account, except where strictly necessary (e.g. bootstrapping access prior to RBAC being fully available)
input_row.rationale: The system:masters group has unrestricted access to the Kubernetes API hard-coded into the API server source code. An authenticated user who is a member of this group cannot have their access reduced, even if all bindings and cluster role bindings which mention it, are removed. When combined with client certificate authentication, use of this group can allow for irrevocable cluster-admin level credentials to exist for a cluster. Impact: Once the RBAC system is operational in a cluster system:masters should not be specifically required, as ordinary bindings from principals to the cluster-admin cluster role can be made where unrestricted access is required.
input_row.audit: Review a list of all credentials which have access to the cluster and ensure that the group system:masters is not used.
input_row.remediation: Remove the system:masters group from all users in the cluster. Default Value: By default some clusters will create a "break glass" client certificate which is a member of this group. Access to this client certificate should be carefully controlled and it should not be used for general cluster operations. References: 1. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/pkg/registry/rbac/escalatio n_check.go#L38
input_row.impact: Once the RBAC system is operational in a cluster system:masters should not be specifically required, as ordinary bindings from principals to the cluster-admin cluster role can be made where unrestricted access is required.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_rbac_system_masters_group_usage_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all Kubernetes RBAC ClusterRoleBindings and RoleBindings using the Kubernetes API:
  - Use: `kubectl get clusterrolebindings -o json` and `kubectl get rolebindings --all-namespaces -o json`
- For each binding, inspect the `subjects` field:
  - Check if any subject has `"kind": "Group"` and `"name": "system:masters"`
- Additionally, review all user credentials configured for cluster access (e.g., in kubeconfig files or certificate subjects) to identify any users or service accounts mapped to the `system:masters` group (this may require parsing certificate subjects if client certificate authentication is used).
- Pass Criteria:
  - No RoleBinding or ClusterRoleBinding grants access to the `system:masters` group except for default or break-glass scenarios (which should be flagged for review).
  - No user or service account is a member of the `system:masters` group unless explicitly justified.
- Fail Criteria:
  - Any non-essential RoleBinding or ClusterRoleBinding references the `system:masters` group.
  - Any user or service account is a member of the `system:masters` group without strict necessity.
- Example kubectl commands:
  - `kubectl get clusterrolebindings -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.subjects[]?.name == "system:masters") | .metadata.name'`
  - `kubectl get rolebindings --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.subjects[]?.name == "system:masters") | .metadata.name'`
- The program should output a list of all bindings and credentials referencing `system:masters` for further review.
